items.text: I'm curious about how the human body works and what helps people heal or recover.
I like learning about health topics like nutrition, mental health, fitness, or injury prevention.
I’m interested in clinical or care settings (clinics, hospitals, therapy, wellness).
I enjoy activities where I support or look after someone who is sick, injured, or going through a hard time.
I'm curious about how laws, rights, and rules protect people in real situations.
I enjoy learning about safety, emergency response, or protecting the public.
I’m interested in careers like policing, law, firefighting, or investigation.
I like piecing together what happened using clues, timelines, and evidence.
I enjoy helping someone practice or improve a skill and seeing them get better.
I’m curious about how kids, teens, or groups learn and grow.
I enjoy settings like classrooms, youth programs, or community centers where I help others learn or grow.
I enjoy figuring out people-problems—like how to motivate someone, resolve conflict, or build confidence.
I enjoy building, fixing, or making things with my hands.
I’m interested in how tools, machines, or structures are put together.
I like settings with tools, equipment, machinery, or hands-on making spaces.
I enjoy challenges that involve tools, materials, or equipment—and seeing what I built or fixed when I'm done.
I enjoy figuring out how devices, software, or systems work behind the scenes.
I’m interested in engineering, coding, robotics, or design.
I like tech-focused settings (labs, makerspaces, computers, equipment).
I enjoy logic puzzles, data patterns, and problem-solving challenges.
I'm interested in how businesses and organizations grow, improve, and make decisions.
I enjoy planning projects, setting goals, and seeing measurable results.
I like leadership settings (teams, clubs, organizing, running things).
I enjoy solving system problems—like improving how something works, is organized, or uses resources.
I enjoy creating stories, visuals, music, or content that shares an idea or feeling with others.
I’m interested in media, communication, design, or performance.
I like creative settings (studio, stage, writing, filming, editing, design).
I enjoy creating something original—a design, story, image, song, or performance—and sharing it with others.
I enjoy fast-paced places where I help people, keep things moving, and solve small problems on the spot.
I’m interested in hospitality, events, travel, food, retail, or customer service.
I like people-facing settings where teamwork, timing, and communication matter.
I enjoy helping someone have a better experience by solving small problems.
I enjoy being outdoors and working directly with nature, animals, or the land.
I'm curious about how ecosystems, wildlife, weather, and environmental systems work.
I like outdoor settings like parks, farms, rivers, trails, or conservation areas.
I enjoy hands-on nature work like growing, caring for, restoring, or protecting natural areas.
I can notice early warning signs that someone's health or safety is at risk and suggest a safe next step.
I can help someone feel calmer or more at ease when they're stressed, upset, or unwell.
I can follow careful procedures—like checking, documenting, or cleaning—so mistakes don't happen.
I can stay focused on details even when the situation feels serious or emotional.
I can stay calm and communicate clearly when there is conflict, urgency, or pressure.
I can explain a rule or boundary in a way that feels fair and firm without being harsh.
I can gather facts, check details, and describe events in the right order.
I can make quick, safety-first decisions when something unexpected happens.
I can break a skill down into clear, small steps that someone else can actually follow and learn.
I can encourage someone who’s struggling without judging them.
I can plan a simple activity, lesson, or group session so people know what to do and why.
I can notice what a person needs (support, challenge, or a break) and adjust my approach.
I can build, assemble, or repair something by reading a plan, following steps, and checking my work.
I can work with a partner on a hands-on job and communicate clearly about next steps.
I can measure, line up, and fine-tune details so the final result fits and works.
I can troubleshoot a problem by testing different fixes safely until one works.
I can analyze a technical problem by breaking it into smaller parts and testing each one to find the answer.
I can explain a tech idea simply so others understand what’s happening.
I can test a system, find the cause of an issue, and improve it using evidence.
I can learn a new tool by experimenting, reading directions, and trying again.
I can identify what a team or project needs, organize a plan to get there, and keep people on track until it's finished.
I can give a group clear direction and help people stay focused and on track.
I can compare options (time, cost, impact) and choose the best one.
I can spot inefficiencies in a business process or team workflow and suggest a practical fix that saves time or resources.
I can produce something that communicates an idea—through writing, design, video, music, or art.
I can match my message to the audience so it makes sense to them.
I can improve my work by revising and polishing, not just finishing fast.
I can share or present my work clearly in front of others, even if I feel some nerves.
I can welcome people warmly and quickly help them feel comfortable and at ease.
I can handle a complaint by listening, staying calm, and finding a fair fix.
I can stay organized across multiple tasks in a busy or active setting.
I can notice what needs to happen next (restock, clean, reset, or update) without being told.
I can care for plants or animals by noticing needs and following routines.
I can work safely outdoors by paying attention to weather, tools, and risks.
I can watch nature carefully and record changes, patterns, or signs of impact over time.
I make choices that protect land, water, and living things—reduce waste, prevent damage, follow rules.
I feel fulfilled when my work directly helps someone heal, recover, or stay healthier.
I'm motivated when I help someone recover from illness, injury, or a health crisis.
I'm motivated to keep learning so I can give better care and catch things other people might miss.
I’m motivated when I keep learning skills that make my care more effective.
I feel a strong sense of purpose when I help prevent harm or protect people from danger.
It matters deeply to me to stand up for what's fair—even when it's hard.
I want to be the kind of person who stays steady under pressure.
I’m motivated when my work improves trust and safety in the community.
I feel fulfilled when someone grows more confident or capable because I helped them.
It matters to me that people feel supported and respected while they're learning, not judged.
I feel motivated when I help someone see a path forward—whether that's a student finding their confidence or a person getting connected to help they need.
I'm motivated when I can contribute to stronger, more connected groups or communities over time.
I feel proud when I build or fix something people can rely on every day.
It matters to me to do quality work that lasts and keeps people safe.
I want to be known as dependable, practical, and good with my hands.
I'm motivated when I master a new trade skill—like welding a better joint, reading a blueprint faster, or operating new equipment.
I feel proud when I finish work that is accurate, reliable, and clearly worth the effort.
It matters to me to improve systems so they are safer, smarter, or more efficient.
I want to be someone who keeps learning and stays current as technology and methods evolve.
I’m motivated when my ideas create long-term improvements, not just quick fixes.
I feel purpose when a plan I helped create—a business goal, project budget, or team strategy—actually works and shows results.
It matters to me to make wise choices with time, money, and resources.
I want to lead in a way that is honest and fair—getting results without cutting corners.
I'm motivated when I help grow something bigger than myself—a business, a program, a team—that keeps running and thriving over time.
I feel fulfilled when something I created helps people understand, feel, or see something in a new way.
It matters to me that my creative work—writing, design, or visuals—communicates an idea so clearly that people immediately feel or understand it.
I want to create work that feels true to who I am, not copied from others.
I'm motivated when I keep improving my craft and feel my work getting stronger over time.
I feel fulfilled when people feel welcomed, respected, and cared for.
It matters to me to solve problems calmly so others have a better experience.
I want to be the kind of person who stays kind and helpful under stress.
I'm motivated when a place runs smoothly, people are satisfied, and I played a part in making that happen.
It matters to me to protect nature and keep land and water healthy for the future.
I feel connected and energized when I work outdoors and can sense the natural world around me.
I want to be someone who actively works to protect ecosystems, reduce harm to animals, and leave the land in better shape than I found it.
I'm motivated by work that supports long-term sustainability, even when results take time to show.
I'm comfortable following detailed steps and procedures, even when they feel repetitive.
I like work that has clear standards I can measure my performance against.
I’m okay starting without clear steps and figuring it out as I go.
I enjoy days where I have to respond quickly to what comes up.
I like being in situations where I have to act quickly, even if it means I might make a small mistake.
I do my best work when I’m not rushed and can take my time to think.
I strongly prefer to avoid conflict or tense situations whenever possible.
I'm okay repeating a process over and over to get really skilled at it.
I'm comfortable working within a clear chain of command where roles and authority are defined.
I like jobs where talking with people is a big part of the day.
I do my best work in a quieter setting where I don't have to interact constantly.
I believe rules and standards should be applied consistently—even when it's unpopular.
I prefer working closely with teammates throughout the day rather than doing most of my work independently.
I prefer doing my part independently and checking in later.
I like solving problems by talking them through with others and building on each other's ideas.
I do my best work when I can choose my own approach to getting something done.
I do best when someone gives me clear steps to follow.
I like being responsible for the whole outcome, not just one piece.
I can stay composed and think clearly when the stakes feel high.
When things get urgent, it’s hard for me to stay steady and think clearly.
I can keep making clear-headed decisions even when the emotional stakes are high—like when someone is angry or in trouble.
I can stay calm and respectful during a tense or confrontational situation with another person.
Frequent interruptions make it hard for me to do my best work.
I can keep my attention on my task even in a busy place.
I prefer work where I’m moving around, not sitting all day.
I prefer desk-based work where I’m mostly in one place.
I don’t mind working in weather or changing outdoor conditions.
I prefer solving problems using facts, data, or careful planning rather than gut instinct.
I prefer hands-on tasks like building, fixing, or operating equipment.
I prefer solving problems by creating something new—like sketching a solution, writing a plan, or designing a better process.
I like receiving feedback during a project so I can adjust and improve as I go.
I remember details well and can write down what happened clearly and accurately.
Regular coaching or check-ins help me do my best.
Even when it takes longer, I prefer doing something the right way over the fast way.
I find it hard to hold firm boundaries or enforce rules when someone pushes back.
I double-check details before declaring something finished.

pathDescriptions.Clinical Care Path: Helping people heal through nursing, therapy, medicine, or mental health care.
pathDescriptions.Protection Path: Keeping communities safe through policing, firefighting, emergency response, or investigations.
pathDescriptions.Learning & Support Path: Helping others learn, grow, and overcome challenges through teaching, counseling, or coaching.
pathDescriptions.Build & Fix Path: Building, repairing, and maintaining physical things using tools, equipment, and skilled techniques.
pathDescriptions.STEM Systems Path: Solving real-world problems by designing, testing, and improving technical systems.
pathDescriptions.Business Growth & Leadership Path: Organizing people, plans, and resources to reach goals and grow organizations.
pathDescriptions.Creative Path: Creating designs, stories, media, music, or content that connects with people.
pathDescriptions.Experience & Service Path: Delivering great experiences through hospitality, food service, events, or customer service.
pathDescriptions.Outdoor Systems Path: Working outdoors with land, water, animals, and natural systems.
